title: Finite FG recursion scaffold
summary: Packages a family of coefficients declared to agree with a finite metric jet and combines it with stored finite-order FG conditions; no recursive solver is implemented yet.

mathematicalContent.heading: What the recursion object stores
mathematicalContent.body: FormalRicciRecursion contains a coefficient family called solvedCoeff and a proof that every one of those coefficients equals the already stored coefficient of J. It provides stable accessors for future recursion calculations, but currently contains no recurrence formula.

mathematicalContent.heading: Finite solved package
mathematicalContent.body: FGRicciSolvedToOrder pairs SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder with a FormalRicciRecursion. The firstJet theorem therefore follows from the first-jet field already stored in SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This structure supplies coefficients ĝ⁽ᵐ⁾ and requires them to agree pointwise with J's existing coefficients g⁽ᵐ⁾.
For m ≤ K, solvedAt accesses the m-th coefficient stored in the recursion object.
correspondence.leanType: def solvedAt (R : FormalRicciRecursion (ι := ι) J) (m : ℕ) (hm : m ≤ K) : Sym2Component ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zeroth solved coefficient equals the base metric because solved coefficients agree with the jet and g⁽⁰⁾ = g.
correspondence.leanType: theorem solvedAt_zero (R : FormalRicciRecursion (ι := ι) J) : R.solvedAt 0 (Nat.zero_le K) = J.baseMetric

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Under the stored order-one FG condition, the first solved coefficient equals twice the stored Schouten tensor.
correspondence.leanType: theorem solved_firstJet_eq_two_schouten (R : FormalRicciRecursion (ι := ι) J) (H : SatisfiesFGRicciToOrder J 1) (i j : ι) : R.solvedAt 1 H.order_le i j = 2 * J.schouten i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This data package consists of stored FG conditions through an order r and a recursion object whose coefficients agree with J.
correspondence.leanType: structure FGRicciSolvedToOrder (J : FormalMetricJet ι K) (order : ℕ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An order-one solved package exposes the same first coefficient relation ĝ⁽¹⁾ = 2P.
correspondence.leanType: theorem FGRicciSolvedToOrder.firstJet (S : FGRicciSolvedToOrder (ι := ι) J 1) (i j : ι) : S.recursion.solvedAt 1 S.conditions.order_le i j = 2 * J.schouten i j

references.locator: Chapter 3, recursive determination of the normal-form expansion
references.note: Mathematical motivation for the future finite-order recursion represented by this scaffold.

limitations: No Ricci recurrence, coefficient-solving equation, existence, uniqueness, parity range, or exceptional-order analysis is implemented. The object named solvedCoeff is required only to equal coefficients that were already supplied in J.
